personal.name: Mrwan Amr Mohamed
personal.title: AI & Data Science Student | Grade 11
personal.summary: Highly ambitious Grade 11 Scientific-Mathematics student bridging hardware engineering and Artificial Intelligence. Proven track record in elite national and international innovation challenges, demonstrating advanced problem-solving skills and the ability to deploy real-time automation systems.

ai_deep_learning.title: Deep Learning & AI Foundations
ai_deep_learning.description: Advanced understanding of neural network mechanics, specializing in building architectures from scratch and hyperparameter optimization based on intensive research and curriculum paths.
items: Core Architectures: Hands-on implementation of ConvNeXt, EfficientNet, CoAtNet, MobileNet, and Vision Transformers (ViT).
Deep Learning Mechanics: Multi-head attention mechanisms, patch embedding, feature extraction pipelines, and building neural networks from scratch.
Frameworks & Libraries: Highly proficient in PyTorch, TensorFlow, Keras, Scikit-learn, and MediaPipe.
Training Loops: Expert in writing custom training loops, managing batch processing, backpropagation, and loss optimization algorithms (Adam, SGD).

computer_vision.title: Computer Vision & Real-time Inference
computer_vision.description: Processing dynamic visual inputs and deploying state-of-the-art models for real-time tracking, object classification, and spatial awareness.
items: Object Detection: Real-time inference deployment using custom trained YOLO models.
Live Tracking: Integrating OpenCV pipelines with DeepSort for multi-object tracking and continuous ID logging.
Data Pipelines: Building custom image preprocessing and advanced data augmentation pipelines using OpenCV.

web_integration.title: Web & Deployment Integration (Foundational)
web_integration.description: Intermediate development capabilities focused primarily on deploying machine learning models and constructing telemetry interfaces.
items: Backend Routing: Creating lightweight, functional RESTful APIs using Flask to serve models.
Web Security: Foundational input sanitization via Bleach and endpoint rate-limiting using Flask-Limiter.
Data UI/UX: Constructing responsive, glassmorphic monitoring dashboards utilizing HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, and Chart.js.

embedded_iot.title: Embedded Systems & IoT Basics
embedded_iot.description: Interfacing localized physical hardware environments with automation software networks.
items: Microcontrollers: Foundational programming of Arduino and ESP8266 NodeMCU architectures using C/C++.
Hardware Control: Basic wiring circuits using relays, MOSFETs, and transistors for structural hardware isolation.
IoT Architecture: Hosting localized web servers directly on microcontrollers to manage hardware-to-web control channels.

competitions.title: Intel ISEF - National Finals
competitions.description: Advanced through intense preliminary and local regional fairs to rank among the top 300 projects nationwide out of over 10,000 elite entries. Demonstrated rigorous scientific research methodology, system engineering, and high-tier project defense.

competitions.title: Saudi AI Championship (SCAI)
competitions.description: Successfully advanced to the national finals, demonstrating competitive expertise in complex AI application design, real-world algorithmic optimization, and international-standard technical pitching under pressure.

competitions.title: DECI Summer Bootcamp - Cohort 3 Level 2
competitions.description: Participated in a high-pressure summer bootcamp mentored by Eng. Mohamed Fathi Mousa. Collaborated with an elite cross-functional team under extreme timeline constraints to engineer a product concept, successfully delivering technical implementation and pitching to domain experts.

competitions.title: Digitopia Innovation Challenge
competitions.description: Engaged as Team Leader in a month-long intensive innovation program. Led a dedicated cross-functional development team through three systematic validation and engineering stages to refine the technical infrastructure of the NeuraEdu project.

competitions.title: NASA Space Apps Challenge
competitions.description: Collaborated within a rapid prototyping engineering team during an accelerated 48-hour global hackathon, developing technical solutions for the specialized 'World-Away' challenge criteria under strict time limitations.

competitions.title: DECI Summer Bootcamp - Cohort 2 Level 1
competitions.description: Completed an intensive engineering bootcamp managed by El Mentor and supervised by Dr. Mahmoud Derar. Engineered a functional software application linked directly with a structured business model MVP in an intense 5-day cycle, defending the presentation directly before an expert evaluation jury.
